question: A 45-year-old man presents with crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm for 2 hours. ECG shows ST elevation in V1-V4. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate management?
A 28-year-old woman is found to have a serum calcium of 11.8 mg/dL. PTH is elevated. She is asymptomatic. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old smoker presents with hemoptysis and weight loss. Chest X-ray shows a hilar mass. Biopsy reveals small cells with scant cytoplasm and neuroendocrine features. Which paraneoplastic syndrome is most associated?

options.C: Cushing syndrome due to ectopic ACTH

question: A 3-year-old boy has recurrent sinopulmonary infections since infancy. Labs show absent B cells, very low immunoglobulins, normal T cells. What is the diagnosis?
A 70-year-old woman develops sudden-onset severe headache described as 'the worst headache of my life.' CT head is negative. What is the next step?
A 35-year-old man with HIV (CD4 80) develops fever, headache, and altered mental status. CSF India ink stain is positive. What is the treatment?
A newborn has failure to pass meconium in the first 48 hours, abdominal distension, and bilious vomiting. Rectal biopsy shows absence of ganglion cells. Diagnosis?
A 50-year-old woman on long-term lithium therapy presents with polyuria and polydipsia. Urinalysis shows low specific gravity unresponsive to desmopressin. What is the diagnosis?
A 25-year-old presents with a painless penile ulcer with a clean base and raised edges. VDRL is reactive. What is the first-line treatment?

options.B: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM single dose

question: A 40-year-old woman has fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, and constipation. TSH is 12 mIU/L. Free T4 is low. What is the treatment?
A 65-year-old man with COPD has worsening dyspnea and productive cough. ABG shows pH 7.32, PaCO2 58, PaO2 55. What is the most appropriate oxygen target?
A 22-year-old woman has episodic palpitations, diaphoresis, and hypertension. 24-hour urine shows elevated catecholamines. What is the diagnosis?
A 55-year-old man with hepatitis C cirrhosis has a new 3cm hepatic lesion with arterial enhancement and washout on MRI. AFP is 450. What is the diagnosis?
A 30-year-old woman presents with unilateral visual loss, optic neuritis, and a prior episode of limb weakness. MRI shows periventricular white matter lesions. Diagnosis?
A 6-year-old has periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows 4+ protein, no hematuria. Renal biopsy shows normal light microscopy but effacement of foot processes on EM. Diagnosis?
An 80-year-old woman falls and cannot bear weight on her left leg. X-ray shows a femoral neck fracture. She is otherwise healthy. Best management?
A patient taking isoniazid for TB develops peripheral neuropathy. What is the mechanism and treatment?

options.A: Isoniazid causes folate deficiency; treat with folic acid
options.B: Isoniazid inhibits pyridoxine metabolism; treat with vitamin B6
options.C: Isoniazid causes B12 deficiency; treat with cyanocobalamin
options.D: Isoniazid causes thiamine depletion; treat with thiamine

question: A 45-year-old woman has a 2cm thyroid nodule. FNA shows papillary carcinoma. What is the recommended surgery?
A 32-year-old woman at 34 weeks gestation has BP 158/104 and proteinuria 2+. She has a headache and visual changes. Diagnosis and treatment?

options.B: Severe preeclampsia; IV magnesium sulfate and delivery

question: A 58-year-old man has progressive dysphagia to solids then liquids over 6 months and 10kg weight loss. Barium swallow shows an irregular narrowing. What is the most likely diagnosis?
